title: BMW Welt

infobox.value: * [BMW](/wiki/BMW "BMW")   + [Alpina](/wiki/Alpina "Alpina")   + [BMW i](/wiki/BMW_i "BMW i")   + [BMW M](/wiki/BMW_M "BMW M") * [BMW Motorrad](/wiki/BMW_Motorrad "BMW Motorrad") * [Mini](/wiki/Mini_(marque) "Mini (marque)") * [Rolls-Royce](/wiki/Rolls-Royce_Motor_Cars "Rolls-Royce Motor Cars") * [Zinoro](/wiki/Zinoro "Zinoro") * [Riley](/wiki/Riley_Motor "Riley Motor") (inactive) * [Triumph](/wiki/Triumph_Motor_Company "Triumph Motor Company") (inactive)

* [Rapp Motorenwerke](/wiki/Rapp_Motorenwerke "Rapp Motorenwerke") * [Automobilwerk Eisenach](/wiki/Automobilwerk_Eisenach "Automobilwerk Eisenach") * [BMW Marine](/wiki/BMW_Marine "BMW Marine") * [Dixi](/wiki/BMW_Dixi "BMW Dixi") * [Glas](/wiki/Glas_(company) "Glas (company)") * [Otto Flugmaschinenfabrik](/wiki/Otto_Flugmaschinenfabrik "Otto Flugmaschinenfabrik") * [Husqvarna Motorcycles](/wiki/Husqvarna_Motorcycles "Husqvarna Motorcycles") * [Rover Group](/wiki/Rover_Group "Rover Group") * [Standard](/wiki/Standard_Motor_Company#BMW "Standard Motor Company")

* [BMW Bank](/wiki/BMW_Bank "BMW Bank") * [BMW M Motorsport](/wiki/BMW_M_Motorsport "BMW M Motorsport") * [BMW in the United States](/wiki/BMW_in_the_United_States "BMW in the United States") * [BMW Mexico](/wiki/BMW_Mexico "BMW

is a combined exhibition, delivery, adventure museum, and event venue located in [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich")'s district [Am Riesenfeld](/wiki/Am_Riesenfeld "Am Riesenfeld"), next to the [Olympic Park](/wiki/Olympiapark_(Munich) "Olympiapark (Munich)"), in the immediate vicinity of the [BMW Headquarters](/wiki/BMW_Headquarters "BMW Headquarters") and factory. It was built from August 2003 to summer 2007. A solar system with 800 kW of power is installed on the roof of the main building. The opening took place on 17 October 2007. The BMW Welt is the most visited tourist attraction in Bavaria.[[1]](#cite_note-sueddeutsche_kronewiter-1)

Kronewiter, Thomas (February 4, 2014). ["Sehenswürdigkeiten in Bayern: BMW-Welt beliebter als Schloss Neuschwanstein"](https://www.sueddeutsche.de/muenchen/sehenswuerdigkeiten-in-bayern-bmw-welt-beliebter-als-schloss-neuschwanstein-1.1878973) [Sights in Bavaria: BWM Welt is more popular than the Neuschwanstein Castle]. *[Süddeutsche Zeitung](/wiki/S%C3%BCddeutsche_Zeitung "Süddeutsche Zeitung")* (in German). Retrieved April 10, 2019.

["BMW AG: Geschichte: BMW Classic"](https://web.archive.org/web/20080517150152/http://www.bmw.com/generic/com/de/fascination/discover/history/mobiletradition/index.html). Archived from [the original](http://www.bmw.com/generic/com/de/fascination/discover/history/mobiletradition/index.html) on 2008-05-17. Retrieved 2008-01-22.

Schmitt, Bernd; Van Zutphen, Glenn (2012), [*Happy Customers Everywhere: How Your Business Can Profit from the Insights of Positive Psychology*](https://books.google.com/books?id=UPi7nca0jukC&pg=PA64), Macmillan, p. 64, [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-1-137-00046-0](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-1-137-00046-0 "Special:BookSources/978-1-137-00046-0")
Rudolf Stumberger und Miriam Zerbel (2007-10-15). ["Bauen für die Zukunft"](https://www.welt.de/muenchen/article1267819/Bauen_fuer_die_Zukunft_.html). Die Welt. Retrieved 2013-06-29.
"BMW Welt attracted almost 3,000,000 visitors last year". *[Roundel](/wiki/Roundel_(magazine) "Roundel (magazine)")*. **46** (4): 46. April 2014.

["COOP HIMMELB(L)AU BMW Welt project records"](https://www.cca.qc.ca/en/archives/449937/coop-himmelblau-bmw-welt-project-records). *[Canadian Centre for Architecture](/wiki/Canadian_Centre_for_Architecture "Canadian Centre for Architecture")*. Retrieved April 8, 2020.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [48°10′37″N 11°33′24″E﻿ / ﻿48.17694°N 11.55667°E﻿ / 48.17694; 11.55667](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=BMW_Welt&params=48_10_37_N_11_33_24_E_type:landmark)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Building in Munich, Germany

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ce/BMW_Welt%2C_M%C3%BAnich%2C_Alemania16.jpg/250px-BMW_Welt%2C_M%C3%BAnich%2C_Alemania16.jpg)](/wiki/File:BMW_Welt,_M%C3%BAnich,_Alemania16.jpg)

BMW Welt building

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b3/M%C3%BCnchen_-_BMW-Werk_in_2018-07.jpg/250px-M%C3%BCnchen_-_BMW-Werk_in_2018-07.jpg)](/wiki/File:M%C3%BCnchen_-_BMW-Werk_in_2018-07.jpg)

Aerial photograph of the BMW Welt, [BMW Museum](/wiki/BMW_Museum "BMW Museum"), [BMW Headquarters](/wiki/BMW_Headquarters "BMW Headquarters"), and BMW factory

The **BMW Welt** is a combined exhibition, delivery, adventure museum, and event venue located in [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich")'s district [Am Riesenfeld](/wiki/Am_Riesenfeld "Am Riesenfeld"), next to the [Olympic Park](/wiki/Olympiapark_(Munich) "Olympiapark (Munich)"), in the immediate vicinity of the [BMW Headquarters](/wiki/BMW_Headquarters "BMW Headquarters") and factory. It was built from August 2003 to summer 2007. A solar system with 800 kW of power is installed on the roof of the main building. The opening took place on 17 October 2007. The BMW Welt is the most visited tourist attraction in Bavaria.[[1]](#cite_note-sueddeutsche_kronewiter-1)

## Operations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BMW_Welt&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Operations")]

BMW Welt operations are coordinated with the other local BMW facilities, the [BMW Museum](/wiki/BMW_Museum "BMW Museum") and [BMW Headquarters](/wiki/BMW_Headquarters "BMW Headquarters").[[2]](#cite_note-2) It has a [showroom](/wiki/Car_dealership "Car dealership") with the current model lineup of BMW cars and motorcycles, and the other two BMW Group brands, [Mini](/wiki/Mini_(marque) "Mini (marque)") and [Rolls-Royce](/wiki/Rolls-Royce_Motor_Cars "Rolls-Royce Motor Cars").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a1/BMW_Museum_%282014%29.jpg/250px-BMW_Museum_%282014%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:BMW_Museum_(2014).jpg)

BMW Museum (2014)

Customers picking up special ordered cars are given a dramatic "staged experience" in which they await their new car in an enormous glass-walled hall, and their cars are lifted up from lower levels on round elevator platforms to their respective delivery area.[[3]](#cite_note-3) BMW Welt also has shops selling BMW-branded [promotional merchandise](/wiki/Promotional_merchandise "Promotional merchandise") and accessories, and a restaurant.[[4]](#cite_note-roundel201303-4)

## Design and construction

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=BMW_Welt&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Design and construction")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d4/BMW_Welt_H%C3%BC.jpg/250px-BMW_Welt_H%C3%BC.jpg)](/wiki/File:BMW_Welt_H%C3%BC.jpg)

BMW Welt in 2005

In 2001, an international [architectural design competition](/wiki/Architectural_design_competition "Architectural design competition") was sponsored by [BMW](/wiki/BMW "BMW"). Twenty-seven offices participated in the competition[[5]](#cite_note-5) four of which were awarded prizes. The jury awarded two offices [Sauerbruch Hutton](/wiki/Sauerbruch_Hutton "Sauerbruch Hutton") and [COOP HIMMELB(L)AU](/wiki/Coop_Himmelb(l)au "Coop Himmelb(l)au") first prize and made the recommendation for the competitors to rework their design submissions in a third design phase in order for BMW to determine which office would be awarded the contract.

The contract was awarded to the Vienna-based architects COOP HIMMELB(L)AU and the facility was constructed from August 2003 through summer 2007 at a cost of US$200 million.[[4]](#cite_note-roundel201303-4) Originally conceived to be open and ready for [World Cup 2006](/wiki/World_Cup_2006 "World Cup 2006"), it eventually opened on 17 October 2007, and deliveries commenced on 23 October 2007. The first customer to take [European delivery](/wiki/European_delivery "European delivery") of a new BMW at the Welt was [Jonathan Spira](/wiki/Jonathan_Spira "Jonathan Spira").[[6]](#cite_note-6)
There were 2,200,000 visitors during the first 12 months of operation.[[4]](#cite_note-roundel201303-4) The number of visitors increased to 2,930,000 in 2013, of which 60% came from Germany.[[7]](#cite_note-roundel201404-7)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/40/BMW_Welt_and_BMW_Headquarters%2C_Munich%2C_Germany.jpg/250px-BMW_Welt_and_BMW_Headquarters%2C_Munich%2C_Germany.jpg)](/wiki/File:BMW_Welt_and_BMW_Headquarters,_Munich,_Germany.jpg)

BMW Welt and BMW Headquarters, Munich, Germany

Designed with an 800 kW solar plant on its roof, "the building does not have the boredom of an exhibition hall, it is not only a temple but also a market place and communication center, and a meeting point for knowledge transfer", said architect Wolf D. Prix at the opening ceremony.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

COOP HIMMELB(L)AU's BMW Welt project records are archived at the [Canadian Centre for Architecture](/wiki/Canadian_Centre_for_Architecture "Canadian Centre for Architecture") in [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal"), [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada").[[9]](#cite_note-9)
